comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS-1 review [E4 Assumption/premise verification] — CLEAN, no findings.

Verified every codebase assertion in the plan via Read/Grep:
- "B-RETIRE removed bespoke orchestrator.run_review, the ONLY producer of per-pass metrics" — corroborated by tests/unit/test_plan_review.py:396-404 and src/rebar/llm/plan_review/workflow_ops.py:9.
- "db7b AC5: coverage['metrics'] = {det_ms, llm_ms, total_ms, llm_calls, claim_path}" — corroborated by test_plan_review.py:403 and src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py:139-141.
- "The workflow plan-review path does not emit them, coverage['metrics'] now absent on the workflow gate" — corroborated by test_plan_review.py:405 ("the workflow path does not yet emit them, so coverage['metrics'] is absent there").
- "the sidecar emits {}" — corroborated by sidecar.py:141 (`.get("metrics", {})` defaults to {} when coverage lacks the key).
- "metrics fed passive latency/cost-target refinement from dogfood data" — consistent with the surrounding docstrings.

All assertions are grounded and accurate; none are unverifiable or contradicted by source.

Affirmations (E4): codebase-assertion verification; fail-closed evidence; named-impl read.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 review — criterion A1 (anti-slop / over-engineering / NIH): CLEAN, no findings.

Affirmations:
- No NIH: the plan reconstructs the db7b metrics from the workflow's OWN per-step recorder `duration_ms` timings (already emitted by the interpreter — see interpreter.py:489/executor.py:672) rather than rebuilding a bespoke `run_review`-style timer. Consuming existing telemetry is the correct reuse.
- No config-surface proliferation: metrics ride the existing `coverage` dict and the existing sidecar lift path (sidecar.py:141); no new config key, flag, or toggle introduced.
- YAGNI satisfied: the metric set (det_ms/llm_ms/total_ms/llm_calls/claim_path) is exactly the retired db7b AC5 field set, serving the current passive-observability done-definition — not a speculative superset.
- FP7 (reference-vs-production): B-RETIRE retired PRODUCTION `orchestrator.run_review` (not a docs/experiments POC); reinstating its telemetry on the surviving workflow gate is the intended lifecycle, so no rebuild-vs-extend NIH applies.
- DSO anti-pattern set (golden-hammer / cargo-cult / resume-driven / premature-optimization): none present — no new tool/pattern forced, the cost proxy mirrors the retired semantics with stated rationale, no trendy tech, and the work is justified by an observed regression, not premature optimization.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 review — criterion T1 (prior-art / novel-architecture justification): PASS, no findings.

Affirmations:
- T1(a) prior art considered: the plan is explicitly grounded in the retired prior art rather than reinventing it — it names the exact prior producer (bespoke `orchestrator.run_review`) and the precise prior contract it reconstructs (db7b AC5: `coverage['metrics']` = det_ms / llm_ms / total_ms / llm_calls / claim_path). The AC pins the equivalence ("equivalent to the retired run_review telemetry").
- T1(b) no novel architecture: this is a reconstruction of a known, previously-shipped contract over the existing workflow recorder, not a new pattern/integration/dependency — no novel-architecture bright-line fires, so no prior-art search is owed.
- T1(c) capability assertion resolved: the load-bearing assertion that the metrics are "captured from the workflow run (recorder step timings / the batch_plan coverage)" names a concrete, existing source mechanism (per-step recorder timings), so it is not an unverified "library supports X" claim.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 review — rubric G1G2 (edit-set / scope accuracy)

AFFIRMATIONS (criteria satisfied):
- [G1G2] Every named edit target exists and is locatable. Verified: the retired producer `orchestrator.run_review` (referenced across `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/workflow_ops.py`, `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py`); the `coverage['metrics']` shape with `det_ms/llm_ms/total_ms/llm_calls` (db7b AC5); the workflow plan-review path (`src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py`); the sidecar (`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py:141` already lifts `coverage.metrics`); and `batch_plan` coverage (`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/pass1.py:302`). No hallucinated targets.
- [G1G2] The named telemetry sources are real and reachable: "recorder step timings" map to per-step `duration_ms` emitted by `src/rebar/llm/workflow/interpreter.py` (set in `executor.py`), and the `batch_plan` coverage is journaled by Pass-1. AC1's source phrasing is grounded.
- [G1G2] Behavioral classification is correct — this is reinstatement of previously-shipped behavior (db7b telemetry retired by B-RETIRE), NOT net-new CREATION, so it is properly in-scope rather than out-of-scope.

FINDING:
- [ ] Enumerate the cross-dir producer dependency in the plan: reconstructing per-pass latency in the gate (`src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py`) requires per-step `duration_ms` to be present on the recorder, which is produced OUTSIDE the plan-review artifact dir by `src/rebar/llm/workflow/interpreter.py` (`_maybe_record_control` / step dispatch) and persisted by `src/rebar/llm/workflow/executor.py`. AC1 names "recorder step timings" as the source but does not call out that the interpreter must time the relevant steps (precheck/agent/batch); if any of those tiers are untimed the metric silently degrades to 0. State whether the interpreter already times all three tiers or whether it is also an edit target. [criteria: G1G2] [location: Acceptance Criteria AC1 / src/rebar/llm/workflow/interpreter.py] [evidence: gate reads `s.get("duration_ms")` at gate_dispatch.py:150; durations are set by interpreter.py:388,489 — a cross-dir consumer the plan does not enumerate]

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 (A1 anti-slop/over-engineering): CLEAN — no findings.

Affirmations:
- No NIH: the plan reinstates metrics by reconstructing them from the interpreter's already-recorded per-step `duration_ms` (src/rebar/llm/workflow/interpreter.py:489-502) rather than building new instrumentation. The det/llm tier split (`_DET_STEP_IDS`/`_LLM_STEP_KINDS` in gate_dispatch.py:117-118) reuses existing step_id/kind data.
- No config-surface proliferation: no new config keys; metrics flow through the existing `coverage` dict and existing sidecar lift (sidecar.py:139-141).
- YAGNI satisfied: each field (det_ms/llm_ms/total_ms/llm_calls/claim_path) maps directly to the db7b AC5 contract being restored — current done-definition, not hypothetical.
- Not a premature abstraction: `_attach_plan_review_metrics` is a single-purpose helper, not a forced generalization.
- The retired `run_review` was the prior production producer (story B-RETIRE), not a designated experiment/POC, so reconstructing equivalent telemetry on the surviving workflow path is the intended lifecycle — not a rebuild-vs-extend NIH concern.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: PASS 1 review (T1 — prior-art / novel-architecture justification): no findings. The plan explicitly anchors on the prior art it reinstates (db7b AC5 coverage['metrics']: det_ms/llm_ms/total_ms/llm_calls/claim_path, formerly produced by the retired bespoke orchestrator.run_review) and proposes to reconstruct an equivalent from the workflow recorder step timings / batch_plan coverage rather than reinventing a telemetry scheme. No T1 bright-line fires (no external integration, new dependency, novel architectural pattern, or unverified capability assertion) — this is a well-trodden reinstatement of an established metrics shape. T1 PASSES.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 review — rubric T8 (LLM/prompt structural-completeness overlay)

This plan defines telemetry on the LLM/agent plan-review gate, so the T8 overlay applies. Two structural under-specifications surfaced, plus affirmations.

AFFIRMATIONS (criteria this chunk passes):
- T8 schema/enum vocabulary: the metrics field set (det_ms / llm_ms / total_ms / llm_calls / claim_path) is fully named in AC1 and inherits db7b AC5's definitions — no undefined-but-referenced vocabulary.
- T8 instruction-locality / referenced-artifacts-exist: the referenced producers (recorder step timings via interpreter `_maybe_record_control`/`duration_ms`, the `batch_plan` coverage, the sidecar `build_payload` metrics lift at src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py:141) all exist and are co-located — no pink-elephant / dangling-reference antipattern.

FINDINGS:

- [ ] Define the `llm_calls` derivation rule in the AC (criteria: T8 — counter/state increment with ambiguous placement). location: Acceptance Criteria, AC1 ("captured from the workflow run (recorder step timings / the batch_plan coverage)"). evidence: AC1 names `llm_calls` as a field to emit but its parenthetical only describes how the *latency* tiers are captured ("step timings"), never how the *call count* is derived. The reference implementation (src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py:156-164) computes it as `finder_criteria` (sum of `batch` step `criteria_count`) `+ agent_calls` (one per succeeded `agent` step). An executor cannot reconstruct this increment rule from the plan text alone — which steps count, and whether a finder fan-out counts as N calls (per-criterion) or 1, is load-bearing for "equivalent to the retired run_review telemetry." suggested_fix: In AC1, state the cost-proxy rule explicitly, e.g. "`llm_calls` = Pass-1 finder `criteria_count` + one per succeeded agent step (verify/coach), mirroring run_review's coverage-derived proxy."

- [ ] Specify the metrics fallback on partial/degraded verdicts (criteria: T8 — unspecified fallback for an incomplete/failed sub-step). location: Acceptance Criteria (whole). evidence: ABSENCE — the ACs cover only the clean PASS path; they say nothing about whether `coverage['metrics']` should be attached when the LLM tier degrades to INDETERMINATE or when only the Pass-4 coach tail fails. The gate has three terminal paths (gate_dispatch.py:90-113): clean success and coach-tail recovery both call `_attach_plan_review_metrics`, but the `_degraded_plan_review_verdict` outage path does NOT, leaving `metrics` absent exactly on the runs the passive latency/cost analysis would most want to flag (slow/failed runs). The plan should state the intended fallback so AC3's test pins it rather than leaving it to implementation accident. suggested_fix: leave empty — the desired behavior (emit a degraded/partial metrics record vs. deliberately omit on outage) is a design choice for the author to decide, then assert in AC3.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: PASS 1 (T8 LLM/prompt structural-completeness overlay): No findings. The metrics schema (det_ms / llm_ms / total_ms / llm_calls / claim_path) is fully specified — value vocabulary defined in AC and grounded in gate_dispatch._attach_plan_review_metrics. No undefined-but-referenced enum/protocol, no ambiguous counter placement (llm_calls proxy = finder criteria_count + agent calls is explicit), and the partial/untimed-record fallback (missing duration_ms → 0, never raises) is specified. This is a telemetry-reinstatement plan, not an LLM-agent design, so the overlay applies thinly and passes.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 review (G6 — approach soundness): The chosen mechanism (reconstruct coverage['metrics'] from the workflow run's recorder step timings, partitioned into a DET tier (precheck) and an LLM tier (agent/batch steps), with llm_calls as a coverage-derived cost proxy) is sound and codebase-aligned — the recorder already times every step, so this avoids re-introducing the bespoke producer B-RETIRE retired. AFFIRMED: mechanism-correctness, fitness-for-purpose, and approach-selection all PASS; an alternative of re-instrumenting each pass inside the orchestrator is structurally different but strictly worse (larger blast radius, reintroduces retired bespoke timing) so the plan's approach is defensible. One coaching finding below on positive rationale.

- [ ] State a POSITIVE rationale in the plan for deriving metrics from the recorder step timings rather than re-instrumenting the passes (G6 §4): the Context explains why the regression matters but not why this mechanism is the right one. (location: Context / AC1)

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 review (A1 — anti-slop / over-engineering / NIH): no findings.

Affirmations:
- No new abstraction or dependency: the latency split rides on the recorder's existing per-step record structure via an ADDITIVE `duration_ms` field (executor._step_record), reconstructed in gate_dispatch._attach_plan_review_metrics from `rec.steps`. No parallel timing facility is invented; wall-clock uses stdlib `time.monotonic()`.
- YAGNI / current done-definition: the metrics serve a stated, concrete need — reinstating the db7b AC5 coverage['metrics'] regression so the sidecar carries det_ms/llm_ms/total_ms/llm_calls/claim_path for passive latency/cost-target refinement. Not hypothetical.
- NIH passes (and is the INTENDED lifecycle): this deliberately reconstructs the dict that B-RETIRE's removal of bespoke run_review dropped, retaining claim_path "matching the db7b dict exactly" rather than rebuilding a competing producer. The `llm_calls` cost proxy explicitly mirrors run_review's coverage-derived proxy (understood reuse, not cargo-cult). `criteria_count` is an existing batch-runner output (production_batch_runner.py:134, runners.py:136).
- No config-surface proliferation: metrics are derived passively from run data; no new config key introduced.
- DSO anti-pattern screen (golden-hammer / cargo-cult / resume-driven / premature-optimization): none observed — plain stdlib timing, scoped to one gate path, no trendy tech, no speculative optimization.

Clean chunk for A1.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 review — G1G2 (edit-set / scope accuracy): CLEAN, no findings.

Verified every file/symbol the plan names resolves in-tree:
- `gate_dispatch.produce_plan_review_verdict` + `_attach_plan_review_metrics` — src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py:46,121
- recorder per-step `duration_ms` on leaf + batch — src/rebar/llm/workflow/interpreter.py:388,489,502,530; threaded through `_step_record`/executor record — src/rebar/llm/workflow/executor.py:655,672
- `precheck` det-floor marker + `agent`/`batch` LLM tier — `_DET_STEP_IDS`/`_LLM_STEP_KINDS` at gate_dispatch.py:117-118
- finder `criteria_count` cost proxy — production_batch_runner.py:134 / runners.py:136, consumed at gate_dispatch.py:157
- `total_ms` measured around `run_workflow` — gate_dispatch.py:73,88
- sidecar top-level `metrics` lift (AC3) — plan_review/sidecar.py:141

Consumer enumeration: the only consumer of `coverage['metrics']` outside the workflow dir is the plan_review sidecar, which AC3 explicitly covers (lift + equality assertion). No unenumerated consumers found.

Scope classification: all hunks are REINSTATEMENT of a previously-existing behavior (db7b coverage['metrics']) regressed by B-RETIRE — in-scope, not out-of-scope creation. No hallucinated/missing edit targets.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 review — criterion E4 (assumption/premise verification): CLEAN, no findings.

Every codebase assertion in the plan was probed by Read/Grep against the live implementation and verified:
- "B-RETIRE removed bespoke run_review, the ONLY producer of coverage['metrics']" — corroborated (run_review now historical; gate_dispatch.py:124, test_plan_review.py:404).
- Interpreter records per-step duration_ms on leaf + batch — interpreter.py:489,502 (_run_leaf) and :388,396 (batch), via executor.py:655,672-673.
- gate_dispatch reconstructs coverage['metrics'] from rec.steps — gate_dispatch.py:121-171, called at :92 (happy) and :102 (coach-failure recovery).
- det_ms = precheck step (a `uses` leaf, plan-review.yaml:24-25, so timed) — _DET_STEP_IDS={"precheck"} (:117).
- llm_ms = agent/batch kinds — _LLM_STEP_KINDS (:118).
- total_ms around run_workflow — :73,88.
- llm_calls = finder criteria_count + agent steps — :156-159,164; criteria_count from production_batch_runner.py:134.
- claim_path structural marker — :165.
- tolerant of partial records (:151 isinstance guard); attached on both paths (:92,:102).
- sidecar lifts metrics — sidecar.py:139-141.

Affirmation: the plan's premises are well-grounded and accurate. No E4 gaps.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS-1 review [T1 — prior-art / novel-architecture justification]: CLEAN, no findings.

Affirmations:
- No T1 bright-line fires that demands a prior-art search: this is a telemetry reinstatement, not a new external integration, unfamiliar dependency, security/auth surface, novel architecture, or migration. The latency-split mechanism (per-step monotonic wall-clock, aggregated by step id/kind into a det vs LLM tier) is a well-trodden instrumentation pattern needing no prior-art justification.
- (a) Prior art is explicitly considered, not reinvented: the plan deliberately reconstructs the EXISTING db7b AC5 contract (coverage['metrics'] = {det_ms, llm_ms, total_ms, llm_calls, claim_path}), states claim_path "matching the db7b dict exactly," and notes the llm_calls proxy "Mirrors run_review's coverage-derived proxy." It anchors to the retired run_review producer rather than designing a fresh shape.
- (b) No novel pattern to justify; moreover the plan documents the SOURCE DECISION (resolving the AC's two candidates — step timings for the tier split, finder criteria_count for the llm_calls proxy), which is exactly the design-choice rationale (b) looks for.
- (c) The load-bearing capability assertion (interpreter records per-step duration_ms) is its own first AC and is verifiable in-tree (interpreter.py records duration_ms on leaf and batch steps; gate_dispatch reconstructs from rec.steps); the claim_path "structural marker (local HMAC verify)" is a marker, not an unverified library-capability claim.

Not a finding — recorded as positive affirmation per coaching spec.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 review (G6 — approach soundness): PASS, no findings.

Affirmations:
- MECHANISM CORRECTNESS: data-flow is complete and verified against the live code. det_ms partitions on step_id `precheck` (confirmed the step id in gates/plan-review.yaml); llm_ms sums agent/batch step durations (interpreter._run_leaf line 489 + the batch finder line 388 both record duration_ms); total_ms wraps run_workflow; llm_calls = batch criteria_count (production_batch_runner emits it) + one per succeeded agent step. Failure/partial cases are handled: a missing duration_ms contributes 0 (never raises), and _attach is invoked on BOTH the happy path and the coach-failure recovery path (gate_dispatch lines 92/102), while the degraded/LLM-unavailable path returns before _attach so the test's `llm_calls > 0` / `total_ms >= llm_ms` invariants only assert when the LLM tier actually ran. No hidden ordering/atomicity hazard (reconstruction reads the recorder after the run completes).
- FITNESS-FOR-PURPOSE: solves the named regression directly — coverage['metrics'] is reattached with the exact db7b five-key dict (det_ms/llm_ms/total_ms/llm_calls/claim_path, claim_path retained verbatim), and AC3's "lifted to the top level" is real (sidecar.build_payload line 141 lifts coverage['metrics'] into payload['metrics']).
- APPROACH SELECTION: reconstructing from generic per-step recorder timings is defensibly at-least-as-good as the structural alternatives I considered (instrumenting each producer/runner to emit metrics directly, or a dispatch middleware) — it has lower blast radius, reuses the existing recorder, keeps the timing capability generic rather than plan-review-specific, and avoids reintroducing the bespoke single-producer coupling B-RETIRE removed. No clearly-superior missed alternative.
- POSITIVE RATIONALE present: "Capture the latency split from recorder step timings... Source decision (resolving the AC's two candidates): step timings for the tier split; the finder step's criteria_count for the llm_calls cost proxy" states why this approach fits.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS-1 review (A1 anti-slop / over-engineering / NIH): CLEAN — no findings.

Affirmations:
- NIH avoided: the plan reconstructs coverage['metrics'] from existing recorder step timings (interpreter.py `duration_ms` on leaf/batch steps, persisted via executor.py `_step_record`) rather than reviving the retired bespoke run_review producer. Reusing the recorder's already-present per-step wall-clock is extend-not-rebuild; reviving run_review would be the actual NIH.
- No config-surface proliferation: the only new surface is two module-private frozensets in gate_dispatch.py (`_DET_STEP_IDS={"precheck"}`, `_LLM_STEP_KINDS={"agent","batch"}`) — internal classification constants, not user/config toggles.
- YAGNI honored: every emitted key (det_ms/llm_ms/total_ms/llm_calls/claim_path) serves the stated done-definition (restore the db7b AC5 dict for passive latency/cost refinement); claim_path is retained to match db7b exactly rather than invented.
- No golden-hammer / cargo-cult / resume-driven / premature-optimization: the duration_ms timing already existed for the batch finder; this extends the same mechanism to leaf steps with a documented rationale (the tier split), and the cost proxy (criteria_count + agent step count) is a deliberate, explained approximation, not blind copying.
- No premature abstraction: `_attach_plan_review_metrics` is a single helper called from exactly its two needed sites (happy path + coach-failure recovery), not a speculative framework.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: PASS 1 (G6 — approach soundness) review: clean. The recorder-step-timing approach is grounded and sound: the interpreter already records per-step duration_ms (interpreter.py:388/489), the batch finder's criteria_count is carried in its recorded outputs (production_batch_runner.py:134), and gate_dispatch reconstructs the tier split tolerantly (gate_dispatch.py:141-171). Mechanism achieves the named goal (reinstate coverage['metrics']), not a proxy; partial/untimed records and the coach-failure recovery path are both handled. Considered an alternative (per-call timing wrappers) — rejected as more invasive vs reusing the existing recorder timings, so the chosen approach is defensibly at-least-as-good on blast-radius/codebase-alignment. Positive rationale is stated in Approach. No findings.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS-1 review [T5c Security overlay]: PASS (not-applicable).

This plan reinstates internal latency/cost telemetry (coverage['metrics'] = det_ms/llm_ms/total_ms/llm_calls/claim_path) on a LOCAL plan-review gate of a git-backed tool. Per the overlay's not-applicable clause, no new security surface is added in this application's actual domain: no new endpoint, no network exposure, no authn/authz boundary, no PII, and no credential/secret/grant. The captured data is non-sensitive numeric step timings + counts plus a fixed descriptive marker string. The `claim_path` marker references that the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify, but this plan neither stores, transmits, nor logs any key/secret material — it only records the constant marker. No findings.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS-1 review [G1G2 — edit-set/scope accuracy]: PASS, no findings.

Affirmations (verified via grep/read against the live tree):
- Every named symbol/file exists: `gate_dispatch.produce_plan_review_verdict` (src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py:46); recorder `duration_ms` on batch (interpreter.py:388/396) + leaf/agent steps (interpreter.py:489/502), persisted by executor.py:672-673 (AC1 source); `precheck` DET-tier step (`_DET_STEP_IDS` gate_dispatch.py:117; plan-review.yaml:24); finder `criteria_count` proxy read at gate_dispatch.py:157, produced in runners.py:136 / production_batch_runner.py:134.
- Out-of-dir consumer enumerated and present: the sidecar top-level lift — `sidecar.build_payload` (src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py:141) lifts `coverage['metrics']` → `payload['metrics']` (AC3), exercised by tests/unit/workflow/test_plan_review_metrics.py:108.
- The offline end-to-end test (AC4) exists at tests/unit/workflow/test_plan_review_metrics.py.
- Behavioral hunks are all in-scope reinstatement of a removed db7b producer; no out-of-scope CREATION of new behavior, and no unenumerated consumers detected. Metrics attached on both the happy path (gate_dispatch.py:92) and the coach-failure recovery path (gate_dispatch.py:102), matching the plan's stated dual attachment.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 review (T8 — LLM/prompt structural-completeness overlay): CLEAN, no findings.

The plan defines a metrics-reconstruction protocol over recorder step records — applied the T8 structural-gap probes and all pass:

- Schema vocabulary fully defined: coverage['metrics'] = {det_ms, llm_ms, total_ms, llm_calls, claim_path}; each key has an explicit derivation and claim_path is pinned to the db7b constant ("matching the db7b dict exactly"). No undefined-but-referenced vocabulary.
- Decision rule co-located with schema: the tier-partition rule (precheck→det_ms; agent/batch→llm_ms) sits with the metric definitions — verified against `_DET_STEP_IDS`/`_LLM_STEP_KINDS` in gate_dispatch.py.
- Counter placement unambiguous: llm_calls = finder criteria_count + one per succeeded *agent* step (verify/coach); the batch finder (kind=batch) contributes criteria_count but not a +1 — stated explicitly, matching `_attach_plan_review_metrics`.
- Failed/incomplete sub-step fallbacks specified: (a) untimed/partial records contribute 0 so the gate never raises; (b) attachment on BOTH the happy path and the coach-failure recovery path.
- Referenced artifacts exist & are fully specified (tool-verified): `precheck` step, the batch finder's `criteria_count` output (production_batch_runner.py:134, runners.py:136), per-step `duration_ms` recording (interpreter.py:489/396, executor.py:672-673), the claim_path constant, and the coach-failure recovery path.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 review (E4 — assumption/premise verification): CLEAN, no findings.

Probed every codebase assertion in the plan via Read/Grep against the live tree; all hold:
- "B-RETIRE removed bespoke orchestrator.run_review, the ONLY producer of coverage['metrics']" — corroborated by gate_dispatch.py:124 docstring + test_plan_review.py references; run_review is now historical.
- "interpreter records a per-step duration_ms (leaf + batch finder)" — verified: interpreter.py:489,502 (_run_leaf dispatch path) and :388,396 (batch finder), threaded via executor.py:655,672-673 (_step_record).
- "gate_dispatch.produce_plan_review_verdict reconstructs coverage['metrics'] from rec.steps" — verified at gate_dispatch.py:121-171 (_attach_plan_review_metrics), invoked on BOTH the happy path (:92) and coach-failure recovery (:102).
- det_ms = precheck step — _DET_STEP_IDS={"precheck"} (:117,152).
- llm_ms = agent/batch kinds — _LLM_STEP_KINDS={"agent","batch"} (:118,154).
- total_ms measured around run_workflow — :73 (_t_total) / :88.
- llm_calls = finder criteria_count + one per succeeded agent step — :156-159,164; criteria_count produced by production_batch_runner.py:134 / runners.py:136.
- claim_path retained as the structural HMAC-verify marker matching db7b — :165.
- "tolerant of partial/untimed records (missing duration → 0, never raises)" — isinstance guard at :151.
- "sidecar carries metrics lifted to top level" — sidecar.py:141 lifts coverage['metrics'] → payload['metrics']; equality asserted by tests/unit/workflow/test_plan_review_metrics.py:108.

Affirmations (E4): (a) each codebase assertion verified by Read/Grep, not training knowledge; (b) fail-closed — no assertion was left unverifiable; (c) ANTI-FP — named implementation files (gate_dispatch.py, interpreter.py, executor.py, sidecar.py) were read directly before concluding. No E4 gaps.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
## Completion Verification — PASS

All four acceptance criteria are satisfied. Evidence grounded in tool output below.

---

### AC1 — Interpreter records per-step `duration_ms` on leaf + batch steps

**MET.**

- **Batch steps** (`interpreter.py:384–396`): `_t_batch = time.monotonic()` before the runner call; `duration_ms = round((time.monotonic() - _t_batch) * 1000, 1)` immediately after; passed as `duration_ms=duration_ms` into `_maybe_record_control` at line 396.
- **Leaf/agent steps** (`interpreter.py:493, 506`): `duration_ms = round((time.monotonic() - _t_step) * 1000, 1)` captured after `_dispatch`; threaded into `_step_record` at line 506.
- **`_step_record` in `executor.py:655–673`**: accepts `duration_ms: float | None = None`; only adds it to the record dict when not None — so absent means "untimed," present means timed. The field is additive and does not break untimed/control-frame records.

---

### AC2 — Workflow plan-review path emits `coverage['metrics']` = {det_ms, llm_ms, total_ms, llm_calls, claim_path}

**MET.**

- `gate_dispatch.py:117–118` — two module-private frozensets classify steps: `_DET_STEP_IDS = frozenset({"precheck"})`, `_LLM_STEP_KINDS = frozenset({"agent", "batch"})`.
- `gate_dispatch.py:121–177` — `_attach_plan_review_metrics(verdict, rec, total_ms)` iterates `rec.steps`, accumulates `det_ms` (precheck duration), `llm_ms` (agent/batch durations), `finder_criteria` (batch `criteria_count`), and `agent_calls` (+1 per succeeded agent step); constructs the five-key dict including `claim_path` = the fixed HMAC-verify marker string; mutates `verdict["coverage"]["metrics"]` in place. Tolerates missing `duration_ms` (isinstance guard at line 157 — missing duration contributes 0, never raises).
- `total_ms` is measured with `time.monotonic()` around `run_workflow` at `gate_dispatch.py:73, 88`.
- `_attach_plan_review_metrics` is called on **both** the happy path (`gate_dispatch.py:92`) and the coach-failure recovery path (`gate_dispatch.py:102`). The degraded/outage path at lines 107–113 does not call it (intentional — the LLM tier did not run).

---

### AC3 — Sidecar carries the metrics lifted to the top level; equals `coverage['metrics']`

**MET.**

- `sidecar.py:139–141`: `"metrics": (verdict.get("coverage", {}) or {}).get("metrics", {})` — the sidecar's `build_payload` unconditionally lifts `coverage['metrics']` into the top-level `payload["metrics"]` key. An absent metrics dict defaults to `{}` (safe for the outage path).

---

### AC4 — Offline end-to-end test asserts metrics are produced AND plausible

**MET.**

`tests/unit/workflow/test_plan_review_metrics.py` (109 lines) drives `produce_plan_review_verdict` fully offline via a `_BranchingRunner(FakeRunner)` that returns schema-valid empty payloads without a model. Two tests:

1. **`test_workflow_gate_emits_coverage_metrics`** (lines 82–99):
   - Asserts `verdict["verdict"] == "PASS"` and `runner.calls > 0` (LLM tier ran).
   - Asserts all five keys present in `coverage["metrics"]`: `det_ms`, `llm_ms`, `total_ms`, `llm_calls`, `claim_path`.
   - Asserts `det_ms >= 0` and `llm_ms >= 0`.
   - Asserts `total_ms >= llm_ms` (total spans the whole run).
   - Asserts `llm_calls > 0` (cost proxy is non-zero on a real LLM-tier run).
   - Asserts `"no-llm/no-network" in metrics["claim_path"]`.

2. **`test_sidecar_lifts_metrics_on_workflow_gate`** (lines 102–109):
   - Calls `sidecar.build_payload(verdict, material="x")`.
   - Asserts `payload["metrics"] == verdict["coverage"]["metrics"]` (equality, not just presence).
   - Asserts `payload["metrics"]["total_ms"] >= 0`.

All five required assertions from the AC are present and targeted at the correct fields.

---

**Verdict: PASS** — every stated acceptance criterion is demonstrably satisfied by the implementation as read from the repository.

compiled_state.description:
## Context

B-RETIRE removed bespoke orchestrator.run_review, the ONLY producer of the per-pass latency/cost metrics (db7b AC5: coverage['metrics'] = {det_ms, llm_ms, total_ms, llm_calls, claim_path}). The workflow plan-review path did not emit them, so coverage['metrics'] was absent on the workflow gate (the sidecar emitted {}). This is a passive-observability regression (the metrics fed passive latency/cost-target refinement from dogfood data).

## Approach (decided)

Capture the latency split from **recorder step timings**: the workflow interpreter records a per-step `duration_ms` (leaf steps + the batch finder), and `gate_dispatch.produce_plan_review_verdict` reconstructs coverage['metrics'] from rec.steps after the run, attaching it to the verdict. Source decision (resolving the AC's two candidates): step timings for the tier split; the finder step's `criteria_count` for the llm_calls cost proxy.

- **det_ms** = wall-clock of the deterministic floor (the `precheck` step).
- **llm_ms** = wall-clock of the billable LLM tier (steps of kind agent/batch: Pass-1 finders + Pass-2 verify + Pass-4 coach).
- **total_ms** = the whole run's wall-clock, measured around run_workflow.
- **llm_calls** = cost proxy: finder criteria_count + one per succeeded agent step (verify/coach).
- **claim_path** = the structural marker (the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify; LLM/network-free) — RETAINED, matching the db7b dict exactly.

Tolerant of partial/untimed records (a missing duration contributes 0) so it never raises inside the gate, and attached on BOTH the happy path and the coach-failure recovery path.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The workflow interpreter records a per-step `duration_ms` on leaf + batch steps (the recorder step-timing source).
- [ ] The workflow plan-review path emits coverage['metrics'] = {det_ms, llm_ms, total_ms, llm_calls, claim_path} (incl. claim_path), reconstructed from the workflow run's recorder step timings + the finder criteria_count.
- [ ] The sidecar carries the metrics again on the workflow gate (lifted to the top level) — verified to equal coverage['metrics'].
- [ ] A test drives produce_plan_review_verdict end-to-end OFFLINE and asserts the metrics are produced AND plausible (all five keys present; det_ms/llm_ms >= 0; total_ms >= llm_ms; llm_calls > 0; claim_path is the structural marker).
compiled_state.title: Reinstate per-pass latency/cost telemetry (coverage.metrics) on the workflow plan-review gate
